edits.oldText: Identity isn't assigned — it's uncovered. Through four progressive phases, students move from discovering what genuinely drives them to graduating workforce-ready with employer-verified proof of who they are and what they can do.
edits.newText: Identity isn't something you find — it becomes visible when you start doing things and paying attention to what you're already capable of. Through four developmental phases, students learn to recognize their own extraordinary capabilities, test where those capabilities create impact, and build a clear direction from that foundation. The program develops identity clarity — knowing your direction and being able to act on it — while building the workforce readiness that connects that clarity to career outcomes.
edits.reasoning: Identity is not uncovered — it becomes visible through action. This is the foundational reframe. Note the additional sentence about identity clarity and workforce readiness.

edits.oldText: Who you are when no one is watching
edits.newText: The capabilities you already have
edits.reasoning: Shifts from introspective/private framing to action/capability framing.

edits.oldText: Uncover What Drives You Discover what genuinely moves you through hands-on activities, conversations with Cleo, and guided reflection. This surfaces your interests, values, and strengths — not what you think you should be, but who you are when no one is watching.
edits.newText: Recognize What You Already Do You already have extraordinary capabilities — skills you practice whether anyone asks you to or not, things you might not even realize are powerful. Through real-world action, reflection with Cleo, and guided activities, this phase helps you see those capabilities clearly and start testing where they create impact.
edits.reasoning: The 'superpowers' concept: students already have capabilities they don't recognize as extraordinary.

edits.oldText: Self-Reported Peer-Confirmed
edits.newText: Surfacing what matters Noticing patterns in behavior
edits.reasoning: Evidence tags describe what the student IS DOING, not a validation tier they've been assigned.

edits.oldText: Target: 70/100
edits.newText: (removed entirely)
edits.reasoning: Identity is not scored. Numeric targets imply linear progression that doesn't match the research.

edits.oldText: Peer-Confirmed Project-Verified
edits.newText: Testing signals through action Interpreting real-world feedback
edits.reasoning: Same principle — action-oriented evidence descriptions, not tier labels.

edits.oldText: Target: 78/100
edits.newText: (removed entirely)
edits.reasoning: Removed — same rationale as Edit 5.

edits.oldText: Project-Verified Educator-Confirmed
edits.newText: Building evidence of direction Connecting to career pathways
edits.reasoning: Practitioner: connecting identity to career direction, not collecting verification stamps.

edits.oldText: Target: 85/100
edits.newText: (removed entirely)
edits.reasoning: Removed — same rationale as Edit 5.

edits.oldText: Educator-Confirmed Employer-Verified
edits.newText: Converged on a clear direction Integrated, validated evidence
edits.reasoning: Professional: convergence on direction, not an endpoint validation.

edits.oldText: Target: 90/100
edits.newText: (removed entirely)
edits.reasoning: Removed — same rationale as Edit 5.

edits.oldText: Guided conversations with Cleo AI to surface genuine interests through behavioral questions — discovering what draws you in when no one's watching.
edits.newText: Guided conversations with Cleo AI to help you recognize the things you already do — the skills you practice whether anyone pays you to or not — and start seeing them as the strengths they are.
edits.reasoning: Recognition language applied to the core Explorer activity.

edits.oldText: Explore how industries are evolving and where different skills create impact. Identify real-world problems that align with your emerging interests. 30 min Reflection
edits.newText: Talk to people working on real problems. Explore where different capabilities create impact. Reflect on which problems energize you — and what that reveals about your strengths. 30 min + action Action + Reflection
edits.reasoning: Changed from passive reflection to real-world engagement. Students talk to people, not just think.

edits.oldText: Discovery Discovery Deep-Dive Curiosity Sprint Rapid exploration of 5 new interest areas in a single session. Try creative, analytical, interpersonal, and kinesthetic activities to find what clicks. 60 min Hands-On
edits.newText: Real-World Real-World Action First Step Challenge Take a low-stakes real-world action — have a conversation with someone in a field that interests you, attend an event, or try something new. Then reflect on what you learned about yourself from the experience. Varies Off-Platform
edits.reasoning: Replaced in-platform exercise with real-world action. Identity develops through doing, not browsing.

edits.oldText: Review your data to identify consistent themes across your interests. Which drew you back over time? Which were fleeting? Patterns reveal identity.
edits.newText: Review your experiences to identify consistent themes. Which activities drew you back over time? Which were fleeting? Patterns in your behavior show you what you're already building toward.
edits.reasoning: 'Patterns reveal identity' is too deterministic. Patterns show direction, not destiny.

edits.oldText: Build portfolio projects that validate your emerging skills. Move from self-reported to project-verified on the validation hierarchy.
edits.newText: Build portfolio projects that test your emerging skills in real contexts. Create tangible evidence of what you can do — and learn what that evidence tells you about your direction.
edits.reasoning: Removed 'validation hierarchy.' Evidence informs direction, it doesn't rank you.

edits.oldText: Map your post-graduation pathway with data-backed confidence. Align college major selection or career entry with your validated identity profile.
edits.newText: Map your post-graduation pathway with clarity and confidence. Align college major selection or career entry with the direction you've built and validated through real-world experience.
edits.reasoning: 'Validated identity profile' sounds like a credit score. Replace with experiential language.

edits.oldText: Complete an internship matched through identity alignment scoring. Earn the highest validation level — employer-verified skills for your portfolio.
edits.newText: Complete an internship matched to your demonstrated strengths and direction. Gain real-world experience that deepens your understanding of where your capabilities create impact.
edits.reasoning: Removed scoring/hierarchy language. Internships deepen understanding.

edits.description: Evidence portfolio — 'scored evidence' → 'evidence of real capabilities'
edits.oldText: Every workshop, reflection, and project produces scored evidence of real competencies. Not a quiz — a portfolio of demonstrated capability that grows with each phase.
edits.newText: Every real-world action, reflection, and project produces evidence of real capabilities. Not a quiz — a portfolio of demonstrated strengths that grows with each phase.
edits.reasoning: Remove 'scored' — evidence is not scored. Remove 'competencies' — capabilities is the correct framing.

edits.description: Validation model rewrite — hierarchy → all-phase availability
edits.oldText: Employers verify awards. Certifications confirm readiness. Each validation level — activity complete, instructor verified, employer verified — raises the stakes and the credibility.
edits.newText: Multiple forms of evidence — self-assessed, peer-confirmed, project-verified, educator-confirmed, and employer-verified — are available across every phase. What changes is the student's capacity to interpret and integrate that evidence into a coherent direction.
edits.reasoning: CRITICAL: ALL evidence types available in ALL phases. The old text implied a validation ladder (activity → instructor → employer). Danielle's rewrite says the hierarchy is the student's growing capacity to interpret evidence, not a tier system.

edits.description: Phase conclusion — 'evidence the market can see' → 'know your direction'
edits.oldText: Explorer to Investigator to Practitioner to Professional. Each phase builds on the last. By the time you graduate, your identity isn't a question — it's evidence the market can see.
edits.newText: Explorer to Investigator to Practitioner to Professional. Each phase builds on the last. By the time you graduate, you know your direction, you can act on it, and you can articulate why.
edits.reasoning: Identity isn't about external proof ('evidence the market can see'). It's about internal clarity ('know your direction'). The outcome is agency, not marketability.

edits.oldText: Explorer to Investigator to Practitioner to Professional. Each phase builds on the last. By the time you graduate, your identity isn't a question — it's evidence the market can see. edits.reasoning: Final CTA: 'discover' → 'recognize capabilities.' Internal coherence, not external proof.

edits.oldText: See how students discover who they are through the 4-phase identity journey — from Explorer to Professional — with real-time skill radar charts and career spectrum matching.
edits.newText: See how students recognize their own capabilities and build a clear direction through the 4-phase identity journey — from Explorer to Professional — with career pathway mapping and evidence portfolios.
edits.reasoning: Radar charts are a visualization, not the product. Replace with what matters: pathways and evidence.
edits._note: demo.html edit — requires file switcher or separate review tool

edits.oldText: Meet Cleo — the AI guide that asks identity-building questions at the right moment, nudges students toward affirming actions, and surfaces career matches they didn't know existed.
edits.newText: Meet Cleo — the AI guide that helps students reflect on their experiences, notice patterns in what they're drawn to, decide what to try next, and surfaces career matches they didn't know existed. A navigator, not a prescriber.
edits.reasoning: Cleo doesn't nudge or prescribe. She helps students reflect, notice, and decide.
